title: Cipocereus pusilliflorus
taxonomy: Cipocereus pusilliflorus is classified under the family Cactaceae. This classification is due to the plant's characteristics, such as its stem and flower structure, which are typical of cacti. Cipocereus pusilliflorus is further classified under the subfamily Cactoideae. The plant's classification under Cactaceae places it within the order Caryophyllales. Its specific classification helps distinguish it from other types of plants.

intro: Cipocereus pusilliflorus, also known as the dwarf pincushion cactus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cactaceae, characterized by its small size and unique pincushion-like appearance. The plant is rich in bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been reported to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its potential health benefits, such as wound healing, antiseptic, and anti-aging properties. The bioactive constituents of Cipocereus pusilliflorus, particularly flavonoids and alkaloids, have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, while the plant's phenolic acids have been reported to possess antimicrobial and antiseptic properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Cipocereus pusilliflorus include infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory infections.
